owner_direction.scope: comprehensive design-only G1 architecture and owner-decision resolution
principles: do not reject disciplined exploration solely for workload, duration, cost, or difficulty
maximize probability of discovering and truthfully confirming genuine future prediction success
treat false confidence, leakage, unverifiable timestamps, backfill, and outcome-aware repair as failure

No capture window overlaps migration
Old store/runtime retained

Incident classification sealed without outcome use

Immediate rule/source/integrity review

Three fixture replays byte-identical
Capture rejects unknown manifest digest

additional_decision_points.question: What end-to-end runtime topology separates prediction generation, capture, evidence sealing, outcome linkage, and evaluation?

pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Separate credentials and network policies for five planes
Capture has no outcome API, result table, evaluator, or recommendation imports
Outcome linker cannot mutate capture
Evaluator read-only over sealed exports

additional_decision_points.question: What monitoring, alerting, and escalation thresholds govern live prospective operation?
additional_decision_points.reason_added: Mandatory G1 domain not completely represented by one of the canonical P274B owner-decision records.
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Heartbeat every 60 seconds; critical after two misses
Warning at T-10 min, critical if no seal by T-3 min, hard fail at T-2 min
Immediate critical on resolver, clock, manifest, digest, storage, access, or capture failure
Primary acknowledgment 5 min, backup escalation 10 min; fail closed regardless

additional_decision_points.question: What security and least-privilege model protects activation, capture, archive, outcome linkage, evaluation, and manual actions?
additional_decision_points.reason_added: Mandatory G1 domain not completely represented by one of the canonical P274B owner-decision records.
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Role matrix and machine identities per plane
No shared write credential; capture write-only, evaluator read-only, linker outcome-append-only
Dual approval for activation, resume, migration, key rotation, retention change, emergency access
Quarterly access review and immediate revocation

additional_decision_points.question: How is the prospective evaluator frozen, separated from outcomes, reproduced, and independently verified?
additional_decision_points.reason_added: Mandatory G1 domain not completely represented by one of the canonical P274B owner-decision records.
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Evaluator and verifier built independently with shared frozen fixtures only
Exact null, varying-N Poisson-binomial, duplicate/idempotency, missingness, and checkpoint fixtures
Evaluator cannot emit efficacy at 50/300
Package/container/source digests sealed before activation and reproducible

additional_decision_points.question: What restart, crash recovery, backup, restore, and disaster-recovery behavior preserves evidence without backfill?
additional_decision_points.reason_added: Mandatory G1 domain not completely represented by one of the canonical P274B owner-decision records.
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Crash at every write boundary leaves zero or one valid deterministic batch
Restart never creates a second valid identity
Backups include all required DB/WAL state through supported online backup or quiesced snapshot, never raw-copy ambiguity
Quarterly sample restore and annual full isolated restore reconcile all digests
Regional/site loss exercise restores read-only evidence before capture resumes

additional_decision_points.question: What state machine governs suspension, resumption, candidate closure, lottery-rule changes, and protocol invalidation?
additional_decision_points.reason_added: Mandatory G1 domain not completely represented by one of the canonical P274B owner-decision records.
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Every transition has trigger, actor, timestamp, evidence digest, allowed next states, and authorization
Lottery rule or ticket-space change immediately suspends project
Integrity breach becomes invalidation when scope cannot be isolated
Candidate closure never shrinks m=3 or permits replacement

additional_decision_points.question: How are official outcome acquisition, availability timestamps, result linkage, and evaluator inputs separated from pre-outcome capture?
additional_decision_points.reason_added: Mandatory G1 domain not completely represented by one of the canonical P274B owner-decision records.
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Separate official outcome source verification and raw-response preservation
Seal barrier proves capture manifest existed before outcome availability
Linker append-only and idempotent; cannot update predictions or seals
Evaluator accepts only verified linked records

primary_criteria: recovery without backfill or outcome leakage
option_evaluation_rubric.decision_rule: Scientific integrity dominates. Complexity, effort, cost, and external dependency risk are tie-breakers only.

decision_records.canonical_question: Proceed to G1 approval, HOLD, or scientific closure
decision_records.governing_reason: The owner explicitly superseded HOLD and authorized exhaustive G1 resolution; a coherent scientifically valid path exists without authorizing implementation or activation.
p274b_evidence: Direct P274C owner direction supersedes prior HOLD
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: P274C decision set approved as the G1 reference
No G2 task starts without a new explicit authorization
invalidation_conditions: Any contradiction with the frozen P274A contract
Any need for retrospective repair or outcome-aware substitution
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Owner may return to HOLD or scientific closure before G2; no data migration is needed because this is design-only

options.name: Approve G1 reference architecture only
benefits: Creates a governed path to future confirmation while preserving separate gates.
disadvantages: Requires substantial pre-G2 evidence and long-term commitment.
options.scientific_integrity_effect: Positive: resolves architecture without weakening protocol.
options.prediction_confirmation_effect: Positive: maximizes the chance a real future edge can be detected truthfully.
failure_modes: Later gates may fail and return to HOLD.
options.reason: Selected by direct owner direction; workload alone is not a reason to reject.

options.name: Continue HOLD
options.scientific_integrity_effect: Neutral but produces no prospective evidence.
options.reason: Rejected because the owner superseded HOLD and a credible path exists.

options.name: Scientific closure now
options.reason: Rejected because evidence does not show every credible path is impossible.

decision_records.canonical_question: Evidence-store architecture
decision_records.governing_reason: A dedicated transactional evidence store plus an independently immutable mirror best separates production operations from confirmatory evidence while preserving atomicity and durable tamper evidence.
p274b_evidence: P271J proves isolated transactional append-only primitives
P271L reports production prospective namespace ABSENT_CLEAN
SQLite WAL requires coordinated sidecar handling
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Choose a dedicated evidence DB technology and WORM-capable independent archive
Prove atomic append, uniqueness, no overwrite, canonical export, restore, and digest reconciliation on synthetic data
Keep the production operational DB outside the evidence trust boundary
invalidation_conditions: Evidence store permits overwrite of sealed records
Mirror is writable by the capture worker
Ledger and mirror cannot be reconciled
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Replace a store only under OD-10 with full digest reconciliation; retain the old store read-only

options.name: P271J schema in production operational DB
disadvantages: Couples evidence to operational writers, WAL, access, backup, and migration.
options.prediction_confirmation_effect: Possible but contamination risk is higher.
options.reason: Rejected because production coupling adds integrity risk without scientific benefit.

options.name: Dedicated append-only evidence database only
options.reason: Rejected as final selection because an independent mirror materially improves auditability.

options.name: Object/file evidence only with manifests
disadvantages: Weak atomic multi-ticket uniqueness and idempotency.
options.scientific_integrity_effect: Can be valid but operational correctness is harder.
options.prediction_confirmation_effect: Risk of missing or duplicate objects.
options.reason: Rejected as sole primary store; retained as archive layer.

options.name: Dedicated transactional ledger plus independent WORM/signed evidence mirror
benefits: Atomic capture, isolation, queryability, and independent tamper evidence.
disadvantages: More components, key custody, archive cost, and reconciliation.
options.scientific_integrity_effect: Strongest proportionate integrity for the long horizon.
options.prediction_confirmation_effect: High: genuine success is detectable while retrospective mutation is exposed.
security_risks: Separate least-privilege credentials and key custody
options.reason: Selected because integrity outweighs added complexity.

decision_records.canonical_question: Official schedule authority and fallback
decision_records.governing_reason: Use an official machine-readable primary source corroborated by a separately fetched official published schedule; disagreement, unavailability, or ambiguous rescheduling fails closed.
p274b_evidence: P274A requires deterministic official target identity
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Document exact official endpoints and manual verification
Store raw responses, fetch times, source versions, Asia/Taipei and UTC normalized times
Pass cancellation, delay, reschedule, ROC/Gregorian, timezone, and disagreement fixtures
invalidation_conditions: Disagreement overridden to create a confirmatory record
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Change source hierarchy only through OD-10 with pre-outcome dual-run evidence

options.name: Caller-supplied draw identity
disadvantages: Identity and close time cannot be independently proven.
options.reason: Rejected because caller assertions are insufficient.

options.name: Single official machine-readable source
disadvantages: Single-source outage or silent schema error.
options.prediction_confirmation_effect: Good when healthy; weak failure detection.
options.reason: Rejected due to unacceptable single-source failure.

options.name: Single official published schedule with cached snapshot
disadvantages: Less structured and slower on exceptions.
options.reason: Rejected as sole source; retained for corroboration.

options.name: Repository schedule with manual exception handling
options.reason: Rejected for confirmatory capture; may only suspend.

options.name: Official machine-readable primary plus official published corroboration and fail-closed exceptions
benefits: Detects disagreement and preserves raw evidence.
options.reason: Selected; manual input never creates a confirmatory record.

decision_records.canonical_question: Trusted clock and maximum drift
decision_records.governing_reason: Use an authenticated multi-source time quorum with explicit telemetry: absolute offset <=1 second, source spread <=2 seconds, telemetry age <=60 seconds, and a hard T-120 second sealing deadline.
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: At least four configured sources, three healthy, two administrative domains
NTS where available; offset <=1 s; spread <=2 s; telemetry age <=60 s
Synthetic skew, stale telemetry, source loss, and clock-step tests fail closed
Batch sealed by T-120 s
invalidation_conditions: Record accepted in unhealthy clock state
Seal time cannot be ordered before outcome availability
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Only tighten thresholds before activation; post-activation semantic change invalidates the family

options.name: Multi-source NTP without authentication
options.reason: Rejected because authenticated sources are proportionate.

options.name: RFC 3161 timestamp as sole authority
disadvantages: Latency and availability are poor for sole scheduling.
options.reason: Rejected as sole clock; optional archive attestation only.

options.name: Authenticated multi-source NTP/NTS quorum plus receipt and seal evidence
benefits: Timeliness, disagreement detection, margins, and audit evidence.
options.reason: Selected for the best availability/proof balance.

decision_records.canonical_question: Maximum tolerated capture gaps and missingness rule
decision_records.governing_reason: Pre-register a bounded envelope without backfill: zero unexplained gaps, suspend on the first miss, at most one consecutive explained candidate-specific miss, and at most 18 explained misses per candidate (0.5% of 3605, rounded down) before insufficient-support closure.
p274b_evidence: P274A requires complete support, no substitution, fixed m=3 and 3605 valid observations
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Automated missing-event schema and pre-outcome deadline event
Sensitivity report for 0, 1, 9, and 18 misses with alpha and m unchanged
Fixtures for candidate-specific and project-wide suspension
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Threshold may only tighten before activation; no relaxation after the first eligible draw

options.name: Any miss invalidates entire project
disadvantages: One isolated failure destroys all candidates.
options.reason: Rejected because bounded candidate-specific gaps can remain future-only.

options.name: Large or adjustable missing-rate threshold
options.reason: Rejected because convenience cannot override integrity.

options.name: Bounded fail-closed envelope: zero unexplained, first-miss suspension, max one consecutive and 18 cumulative explained misses
benefits: Exact behavior, no backfill, unaffected candidates continue.
disadvantages: Requires monitoring and may close a candidate.
options.scientific_integrity_effect: Strong if classification is sealed before outcome.
options.prediction_confirmation_effect: High within a small predeclared envelope.
options.reason: Selected; 18 is a pre-activation ceiling, not a result-triggered extension.

decision_records.canonical_question: Service/on-call/scientific/security ownership
decision_records.governing_reason: Use a named role-based RACI with primary and backup assignees, explicit acceptance, succession, and separation of duties before G2.
p274b_evidence: NIST audit controls support separated accountability
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Named primary/backup for service, operations, science, security/key custody, outcome linkage, and independent verification
Recorded acknowledgment and escalation coverage
No incompatible role combination
invalidation_conditions: G2 begins with an unassigned role
One actor controls generation, outcome linkage, mutation, and verification
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Assignee changes require governed handoff and access review

options.name: Named primary/backup RACI with separation of duties
benefits: Accountability, backup coverage, least privilege, succession.
options.reason: Selected; model resolved now, assignee evidence is pre-G2.

decision_records.canonical_question: Retention and independent evidence durability
decision_records.governing_reason: Retain evidence for the full protocol lifetime plus seven years in an independently administered WORM mirror with quarterly integrity sampling and annual full restore verification.
p274b_evidence: P274B local hashes do not protect against single-store loss
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Retention policy: protocol lifetime plus seven years
WORM/version-lock proof
Quarterly digest sample and annual full restore
Documented deletion authority after retention
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Provider may migrate only under OD-10; retention cannot shorten after activation

options.name: Operational store retention only
disadvantages: Same admins and failures threaten all evidence.

options.name: Protocol lifetime with ordinary backups
disadvantages: Mutable backups and no later audit window.
options.reason: Rejected because later independent review matters.

options.name: Protocol lifetime plus seven years in independent WORM archive
benefits: Durability, later audit, separation from capture operators.
options.reason: Selected because the long horizon justifies durable retention.

decision_records.canonical_question: Signing and key custody
decision_records.governing_reason: Sign every canonical batch and daily archive manifest with an asymmetric key controlled outside the capture worker; preserve verification material and rotation/revocation history.
p274b_evidence: P271J hashes are local tamper evidence only
P274B makes key custody an owner decision
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Non-exportable asymmetric key design
Capture worker cannot administer or export key
Rotation, revocation, compromise, and historical verification fixtures
Independent verifier has public material only
invalidation_conditions: Capture worker can alter verification history
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Rotate only through OD-10 with overlap signatures and old-key verification retained

options.name: No signing; hashes in same store
disadvantages: Privileged mutation can replace record and hash.
options.reason: Rejected because hashes lack an independent anchor.

options.name: Hashes copied to independent mutable storage
options.reason: Rejected because signed WORM manifests are stronger.

options.name: Managed asymmetric signatures on batch and archive manifests
benefits: Independent authenticity, key history, verifier separation.

options.name: External RFC 3161 notarization for every batch
options.reason: Rejected as mandatory baseline; retained for high-assurance profile.

decision_records.canonical_question: Permissible pre-close correction classes
decision_records.governing_reason: Once a valid batch is sealed, predictions are never corrected. Failed uncommitted attempts may retry before T-120 with the same deterministic identity; non-substantive annotations are append-only and cannot alter identity, payload, timing, or eligibility.
p274b_evidence: P271J atomic rollback and append-only amendments
P274A prohibits outcome-aware correction and substitution
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Separate no-commit retry from amendment of sealed evidence
Amendment allowlist excludes substantive fields
Dual approval for annotation; original visible
invalidation_conditions: Correction after deadline or outcome availability
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Policy cannot relax after activation

options.name: Append-only substantive correction before close
options.reason: Rejected because sealed predictions must remain final.

options.name: Mark every error missing; no retry
options.reason: Rejected because deterministic retry before commit/deadline is safe.

options.name: No substantive correction after seal; deterministic pre-commit retry and non-substantive append-only annotation
benefits: Preserves originals and safe transaction recovery.
options.reason: Selected as the narrowest valid policy.

decision_records.canonical_question: Infrastructure migration and dependency upgrade policy
decision_records.governing_reason: Allow only pre-approved reproducible migrations during a capture pause, with old/new dual verification, canonical digest reconciliation, rollback, preserved old evidence, and no semantic candidate/evaluator change.
p274b_evidence: P271K additive install and rollback rehearsal
P271L WAL, backup, quiescence and apply gates
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Reproducible source/dependency manifests
Synthetic rehearsal, rollback, dual-read comparison, and all-record digest reconciliation
No capture window overlaps migration
Old store/runtime retained
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Rollback to preserved version; close affected candidate if pre-close capture cannot be restored

options.name: Freeze all infrastructure for protocol lifetime
options.reason: Rejected as operationally impossible over the likely lifetime.

options.name: Normal in-place upgrades with regression tests
options.reason: Rejected as insufficient for confirmatory evidence.

options.name: Paused reproducible migration with dual verification, immutable old version, and rollback
options.reason: Selected because durability requires governed evolution.

decision_records.canonical_question: Prolonged outage resume/closure rule
decision_records.governing_reason: Any project-wide missed deadline suspends all capture; any candidate miss suspends that candidate. Resume requires explicit incident closure and owner reauthorization before the next eligible draw. No backfill; OD-05 limits force insufficient-support closure.
p274b_evidence: P274A no backfill and insufficient-support class
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Runbook defines project-wide and candidate-specific suspension
Resume checklist proves resolver, clock, storage, version, monitor, and personnel health
Incident classification sealed without outcome use
invalidation_conditions: Automatic resume after a missed deadline
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Before activation owner may close; after activation only stricter closure is allowed

options.name: Never resume after any outage
disadvantages: Harmless pre-deadline outages terminate the program.
options.reason: Rejected because verified pre-deadline recovery can remain valid.

options.name: Fail-closed suspension, explicit incident closure and owner reauthorization, threshold-based closure
options.prediction_confirmation_effect: Preserves future-only evidence when recovery is proven.

decision_records.canonical_question: Cost/resource commitment for 3605 draws
decision_records.governing_reason: Proceed only with an owner-signed minimum resource commitment covering two-person ownership, monitoring, independent archive, key custody, annual restore/audit, dependency maintenance, and migration capacity for the full protocol lifetime.
p274b_evidence: P274A horizon is 3605 draws and about 11.5 years descriptively
Owner direction says difficulty alone is not grounds for rejection
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Signed minimum resource declaration by category and role
Annual renewal without efficacy peeking
Scientific closure if commitments lapse before G2
invalidation_conditions: Archive, monitoring, backup, or security intentionally unfunded
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Owner may choose HOLD before G2; post-activation withdrawal triggers suspension and closure review

options.name: Fund minimum integrity commitments conditionally before G2
benefits: Makes long-horizon operation credible without pretending budget exists.
options.reason: Selected; workload is accepted, commitment evidence remains a hard gate.

options.name: HOLD or close because the program is large
disadvantages: Rejects a credible path solely for workload.
options.reason: Rejected; size alone is not a scientific reason to stop.

decision_records.canonical_question: Periodic non-efficacy scientific-value review cadence
decision_records.governing_reason: Conduct annual governance and scientific-value reviews strictly blinded to efficacy statistics; review integrity, feasibility, rule/source changes, ownership, cost, and continuation/HOLD/closure only.
p274b_evidence: P274B requires value review and rule surveillance
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Template excludes success counts, rates, p-values, conditional efficacy, and ranking
Annual signed continue/HOLD/closure decision
Immediate rule/source/integrity review
invalidation_conditions: Review changes alpha, m, horizon, endpoint, candidates, or boundary
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Cadence may increase if still non-efficacy

options.name: Annual blinded governance/scientific-value review plus event-driven integrity review
options.reason: Selected; annual cadence balances durability and burden.

decision_records.canonical_question: Future G2 implementation scope
decision_records.governing_reason: Define G2 as isolated, disabled-by-default packages for manifests, resolver/time, capture, evidence mirror/export, outcome linker, evaluator/verifier, monitoring/security, and recovery rehearsal. This scopes a possible G2; it does not authorize it.
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Future G2 prompt lists exact files, environments, tests, and forbidden actions
Every conditional pre-G2 acceptance item is evidenced
No production DB, activation, deployment, or apply bundled into G2
invalidation_conditions: G2 includes activation, apply, registry mutation, backfill, or protocol change
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Owner may narrow or decline G2; expansion requires a new G1 amendment

options.name: Consider isolated G2 package only after pre-G2 gates
Monolithic implementation and activation
options.reason: Rejected; activation and apply remain separate.

options.name: Do not define implementation scope
options.reason: Rejected because a bounded package can now be defined.

decision_records.canonical_question: How are P274A candidates, fields, strategy/scorer versions, ticket identities, and dependency provenance sealed and mapped?
decision_records.governing_reason: Use one signed candidate manifest mapping C1-C3 to exact source, dependency, scorer endpoint, input schema, expected ticket semantics, and canonical output fixtures; every capture references its digest.
decision_records.responsible_role: Candidate Manifest Custodian / Scientific Protocol Steward
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Manifest includes protocol, candidate, strategy source, dependency/container, scorer digest, expected N, schema, fixtures
Three fixture replays byte-identical
Capture rejects unknown manifest digest
invalidation_conditions: Ticket identity inferred from strategy name
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Manifest immutable after activation; semantic change requires new future-only protocol

options.name: Source and dependency lock without signed manifest
options.reason: Rejected because manifest binding is proportionate.

options.name: Signed sealed candidate/scorer/dependency manifest with canonical fixtures
benefits: One authoritative identity and reproducibility anchor.

decision_records.canonical_question: What end-to-end runtime topology separates prediction generation, capture, evidence sealing, outcome linkage, and evaluation?
decision_records.governing_reason: Use five isolated planes: outcome-blind prediction producer, resolver/time service, one-shot capture writer, post-outcome linker, and read-only evaluator/verifier; only signed manifests and deterministic IDs cross boundaries.
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Separate credentials and network policies for five planes
Capture has no outcome API, result table, evaluator, or recommendation imports
Outcome linker cannot mutate capture
Evaluator read-only over sealed exports
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Topology migrates only under OD-10 with equivalent separation

options.name: Two planes: capture and evaluation
options.reason: Rejected because finer separation materially improves proof.

options.name: Event-bus pipeline with many microservices
options.reason: Rejected as baseline because complexity adds unsupported risk.

options.name: Five isolated planes with one-shot capture and signed deterministic handoffs
benefits: Clear trust boundaries and least privilege.

decision_records.canonical_question: What monitoring, alerting, and escalation thresholds govern live prospective operation?
decision_records.governing_reason: Use independent monitoring for heartbeat, resolver/time, identity, deadlines, storage/mirror reconciliation, gaps, silence, and access events, with fail-closed escalation.
p274b_evidence: NIST log and audit failure controls
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Heartbeat every 60 seconds; critical after two misses
Warning at T-10 min, critical if no seal by T-3 min, hard fail at T-2 min
Immediate critical on resolver, clock, manifest, digest, storage, access, or capture failure
Primary acknowledgment 5 min, backup escalation 10 min; fail closed regardless
invalidation_conditions: Monitor shares failure domain without independent signal
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Thresholds may tighten before activation; relaxation requires G1 amendment

options.name: Independent deadline and integrity monitoring with primary/backup escalation

decision_records.canonical_question: What security and least-privilege model protects activation, capture, archive, outcome linkage, evaluation, and manual actions?
decision_records.governing_reason: Use deny-by-default per-plane service identities, short-lived credentials, dual control for activation/resume/migration/key actions, immutable audit logs, and independent key custody.
p274b_evidence: P271J actor strings are not authentication
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Role matrix and machine identities per plane
No shared write credential; capture write-only, evaluator read-only, linker outcome-append-only
Dual approval for activation, resume, migration, key rotation, retention change, emergency access
Quarterly access review and immediate revocation
invalidation_conditions: Capture obtains outcome or deletion privilege
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Credential rotation follows OD-10; privilege may only narrow without new G1 review

options.name: Network isolation only
disadvantages: Does not authorize or attribute actions.

options.name: Per-plane identities, short-lived credentials, dual control, immutable audit, independent key custody

decision_records.canonical_question: How is the prospective evaluator frozen, separated from outcomes, reproduced, and independently verified?
decision_records.governing_reason: Freeze a read-only evaluator package and a separately implemented verifier before activation; both consume only sealed prospective exports plus post-seal official outcomes and enforce exact 50/300/3605, m=3, binomial/Poisson-binomial, and no-interim-efficacy rules.
p274b_evidence: P274A defines statistical protocol but no executable evaluator
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Evaluator and verifier built independently with shared frozen fixtures only
Exact null, varying-N Poisson-binomial, duplicate/idempotency, missingness, and checkpoint fixtures
Evaluator cannot emit efficacy at 50/300
Package/container/source digests sealed before activation and reproducible
invalidation_conditions: Semantic evaluator patch after first outcome
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Non-semantic runtime replacement allowed under OD-10 with byte-identical fixture outputs; semantic change requires new protocol

options.name: Implement evaluator at final analysis time
Reuse retrospective analysis code with modifications
disadvantages: Historical assumptions and ungoverned inputs may leak.

options.name: One frozen prospective evaluator
disadvantages: Single implementation bug can decide result.
options.reason: Rejected as final selection because independent verification materially reduces false classification.

options.name: Frozen evaluator plus independently implemented verifier
benefits: Reproducible final decision and independent error detection.
options.prediction_confirmation_effect: High: genuine success and null outcomes are reproducible.
options.reason: Selected to prevent a single evaluator bug from becoming a false claim.

decision_records.canonical_question: What restart, crash recovery, backup, restore, and disaster-recovery behavior preserves evidence without backfill?
decision_records.governing_reason: Use deterministic idempotent one-shot jobs, atomic ledger writes, pre-close retry only when no valid seal exists, independent WORM mirror, encrypted backups, quarterly sample restore, annual full restore, and fail-closed disaster recovery.
p274b_evidence: SQLite WAL sidecars require coordinated handling
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Crash at every write boundary leaves zero or one valid deterministic batch
Restart never creates a second valid identity
Backups include all required DB/WAL state through supported online backup or quiesced snapshot, never raw-copy ambiguity
Quarterly sample restore and annual full isolated restore reconcile all digests
Regional/site loss exercise restores read-only evidence before capture resumes
invalidation_conditions: Restore loses or rewrites sealed evidence
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Recovery tooling may change under OD-10; restored evidence must remain canonically identical

options.name: Ordinary backups and manual restart
disadvantages: Restore and timing behavior are unproven.

options.name: Deterministic idempotent restart plus verified mirror/backup/restore and fail-closed DR
benefits: Recovers without duplicate, overwrite, or backfill.

decision_records.canonical_question: What state machine governs suspension, resumption, candidate closure, lottery-rule changes, and protocol invalidation?
decision_records.governing_reason: Use explicit append-only states with typed triggers: PREACTIVATION, READY, ACTIVE, SUSPENDED_CANDIDATE, SUSPENDED_PROJECT, CLOSED_INSUFFICIENT_SUPPORT, CLOSED_NULL/FAILED/CONFIRMED at final, and PROTOCOL_INVALIDATED. Resumption is never automatic.
p274b_evidence: P274A decision classes and integrity stops
P274B failure matrix and long-horizon rule-change gaps
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Every transition has trigger, actor, timestamp, evidence digest, allowed next states, and authorization
Lottery rule or ticket-space change immediately suspends project
Integrity breach becomes invalidation when scope cannot be isolated
Candidate closure never shrinks m=3 or permits replacement
invalidation_conditions: Statistical failure used to hide integrity failure
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Suspension may resume only by OD-11; invalidated family cannot resume and needs a new future-only protocol

options.name: Pause/resume boolean only
disadvantages: Cannot distinguish candidate/project, closure, or invalidation.

options.name: Any anomaly permanently invalidates all candidates
options.reason: Rejected because scoped suspension/closure can be truthful.

options.name: Typed append-only project/candidate state machine with explicit authorization
benefits: Truthful separation of operational, statistical, and integrity outcomes.
options.reason: Selected to eliminate ambiguous HOLD/failed/invalidated semantics.

decision_records.canonical_question: How are official outcome acquisition, availability timestamps, result linkage, and evaluator inputs separated from pre-outcome capture?
decision_records.governing_reason: Use a post-outcome official-source acquisition service and append-only linker that cannot access capture credentials; linkage is allowed only after a verified evidence seal and stores raw source, availability time, normalized result, and source digest.
p274b_evidence: P271J has outcome-link table but no workflow/API
pre_g2_acceptance_evidence: Separate official outcome source verification and raw-response preservation
Seal barrier proves capture manifest existed before outcome availability
Linker append-only and idempotent; cannot update predictions or seals
Evaluator accepts only verified linked records
invalidation_conditions: Outcome source or availability time unverifiable
migration_or_reversal_conditions: Outcome source migration follows OD-10 and dual-source validation; sealed capture never rewritten

options.name: Separate outcome table but same service/admin identity
Independent official outcome acquisition and append-only linker behind a verified seal barrier
options.reason: Selected to make outcome separation enforceable.

architecture_profiles.summary: A dedicated single-host evidence runtime using an adapted P271J ledger in a separate SQLite DB, one official source plus cached official schedule, multi-source NTP, local signed manifests, and manual primary/backup operations.
architecture_profiles.prediction_generation: Frozen container/source digest; deterministic C1-C3 generation only.
architecture_profiles.capture_sequence: Resolve -> verify time -> generate -> normalize -> atomic append -> sign/export by T-120.
architecture_profiles.trusted_time_sequence: At least three healthy sources; selected thresholds still apply.
architecture_profiles.resolver_sequence: Official machine-readable primary; cached official schedule corroboration.
architecture_profiles.evidence_identity: activation/candidate/draw/strategy/version/bet identity plus batch and ticket digests.
architecture_profiles.storage_topology: One dedicated SQLite evidence DB plus separate local immutable export target.
architecture_profiles.evaluator_flow: Frozen read-only evaluator plus manual independent recomputation.
architecture_profiles.monitoring: Heartbeat, T-10/T-3/T-2 deadlines, critical integrity alerts.
architecture_profiles.access_roles: Named service, operations, science, security, linker, verifier roles.
architecture_profiles.backup_archive: Encrypted backup and local immutable copy; quarterly restore sample.
architecture_profiles.restart_recovery: Atomic idempotent retry before deadline only.
architecture_profiles.ownership_3605: Primary/backup roles and annual blinded review.
architecture_profiles.assessment: Scientifically valid only if all conditional gates pass; not preferred for 3605 draws.

architecture_profiles.summary: Five isolated planes with a dedicated transactional evidence DB, independent WORM/signed mirror, dual official resolver, NTP/NTS quorum, independent monitoring, frozen evaluator plus verifier, and governed long-horizon ownership.
trust_boundaries: capture plane has no outcome access
monitor receives health evidence but no mutation rights
architecture_profiles.prediction_generation: Frozen C1-C3 containers built from signed manifest; no registry substitution.
architecture_profiles.candidate_version_pinning: Source, dependencies, scorer, schema, fixtures, and manifest digest.
architecture_profiles.capture_sequence: Resolve official draw -> corroborate -> validate clock -> generate -> normalize/fingerprint -> atomic ledger append -> canonical batch -> signature -> WORM mirror -> monitor complete by T-120.
architecture_profiles.trusted_time_sequence: 4 configured, 3 healthy, 2 administrative domains, NTS where available; <=1 s offset, <=2 s spread, <=60 s telemetry age.
architecture_profiles.resolver_sequence: Official machine-readable primary plus separately fetched official published schedule; disagreement or exception fails closed.
architecture_profiles.evidence_identity: Deterministic activation/candidate/draw/version/bet IDs, canonical ticket fingerprints, batch digest, signature, archive object version.
architecture_profiles.storage_topology: Dedicated transactional evidence DB plus independently administered WORM object archive and encrypted backups.
architecture_profiles.outcome_acquisition_separation: Independent official-source acquisition after verified seal barrier; append-only linker.
architecture_profiles.evaluator_flow: Frozen evaluator and independently implemented verifier read only sealed exports and verified outcome links.
architecture_profiles.monitoring: Independent heartbeats, deadlines, resolver/time/version/digest/storage/access/gap alarms with primary/backup escalation.
architecture_profiles.access_roles: Per-plane short-lived identities; dual control for activation/resume/migration/key/retention actions.
architecture_profiles.backup_archive: Protocol lifetime + 7 years WORM, quarterly sample verification, annual full restore.
architecture_profiles.restart_recovery: Idempotent pre-close retry only if no valid seal; fail-closed DR and explicit resume.
architecture_profiles.migration: Paused reproducible dual verification, full digest reconciliation, immutable old version and rollback.
architecture_profiles.missingness: Zero unexplained; first-miss suspension; <=1 consecutive and <=18 cumulative explained misses per candidate.
architecture_profiles.suspension_resume: Candidate/project scoped; no automatic resume; explicit incident closure and owner reauthorization.
architecture_profiles.protocol_invalidation: Wrong identity, leakage, unverifiable time, unauthorized mutation, or unisolated breach invalidates affected scope.
architecture_profiles.ownership_3605: Named primary/backup RACI, signed resource commitment, annual blinded review and governed handoff.
architecture_profiles.assessment: Selected because it maximizes truthful confirmation durability without the disproportionate distributed complexity of the high-assurance profile.

architecture_profiles.summary: Replicated event log and transactional ledger, dual-region WORM archives, HSM signing, per-batch RFC 3161 timestamping, independent verifier environment, and two-person operations.
components: two transactional replicas with deterministic commit evidence
architecture_profiles.prediction_generation: Reproducible hermetic builder with provenance attestation.
architecture_profiles.candidate_version_pinning: Signed provenance and reproducible build attestation.
architecture_profiles.capture_sequence: Recommended sequence plus replicated commit certificate and external timestamp token.
architecture_profiles.trusted_time_sequence: Recommended quorum plus external timestamp attestation.
architecture_profiles.resolver_sequence: Recommended dual official sources plus independently archived source snapshots.
architecture_profiles.evidence_identity: Recommended identity plus event-log sequence, replica proofs, RFC 3161 token.
architecture_profiles.storage_topology: Replicated ledger, dual WORM regions/providers, offline verification bundle.
architecture_profiles.evaluator_flow: Two independent implementations in distinct environments with third-party review.
architecture_profiles.access_roles: Two-person rule and separate audit organization.
architecture_profiles.backup_archive: Dual-provider retention and offline key/verification escrow.
architecture_profiles.restart_recovery: Regional failover without changing deterministic identity; fail closed on split-brain.
architecture_profiles.migration: Parallel old/new operation with third-party reconciliation.
architecture_profiles.missingness: Same scientific envelope; more infrastructure reduces misses.
architecture_profiles.suspension_resume: Same state machine plus independent auditor approval for severe incidents.
architecture_profiles.protocol_invalidation: Same triggers; broader evidence may isolate affected scope.
architecture_profiles.ownership_3605: Dedicated funded service and independent audit function.
single_points_of_failure: external timestamp authority policy if not diversified
architecture_profiles.assessment: Maximum auditability, but higher distributed-system and external-dependency complexity is not presently justified by additional scientific integrity over the recommended profile.

recommended_reference_architecture.governing_reason: It provides the best proportionate combination of future-only proof, transactional correctness, independent tamper evidence, failure detection, long-horizon maintainability, and evaluator independence. The minimum profile has unacceptable single-host/store weaknesses; the maximum profile adds distributed/external complexity not presently justified by additional integrity.

component_diagram: See Markdown Mermaid diagram; authoritative component identities are also represented by data_flow and trust_boundaries.

data_flow.action: Load sealed activation and signed candidate manifest
Fetch primary official machine-readable schedule and independent official published corroboration
data_flow.output: versioned target draw and close time

data_flow.action: Verify pre-draw heartbeat, storage, archive, credentials, and owner coverage
Generate C1-C3 predictions from frozen manifest without outcome access
data_flow.output: candidate ticket payloads and generation evidence

data_flow.action: Validate numbers, canonicalize tickets, calculate fingerprints and actual distinct-ticket counts
Validate target, time, manifest, provenance, deadline, and deterministic identity
Atomically append batch, tickets, and capture event
Create canonical batch manifest and digest
Sign batch manifest with externally controlled key
Write signed bundle to independently administered WORM mirror
Confirm valid seal and mirror by T-120; emit capture-complete or missing/suspension event
After official availability, fetch and preserve raw official outcome and availability evidence
Append idempotent result link without modifying capture evidence
At 50 and 300 run integrity/futility-only outputs; never emit interim efficacy
At 3605 valid observations apply exact final tests and fixed m=3 classes

trust_boundaries.allowed_flow: No data-plane write or DB coupling; only sealed source/build artifacts may be referenced
prohibited: operational DB as recommended evidence store

trust_boundaries.allowed_flow: Canonical candidate payload plus signed manifest identity
Signed/versioned target, close, and time-health evidence
trust_boundaries.control: Dual official sources and time quorum
Independent archive credential and key custody

trust_boundaries.allowed_flow: Dual-approved governed command and immutable event

state_machine.entry: Separate explicit G2 authorization after all pre-G2 gates

state_machine.notes: P271 still not activated until explicit authorization.

state_machine.entry: G6 writes activation_timestamp_utc and first_eligible_target_draw before outcome exposure

state_machine.notes: Explicit resume only; m stays 3; no replacement.

state_machine.entry: 3605 valid observations and integrity gates pass

state_machine.notes: Research result only; no production authorization.

state_machine.entry: Unisolated leakage, timing, identity, mutation, or provenance breach
state_machine.notes: Cannot resume; new future-only protocol required.

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Outcome-availability timestamp <= capture seal or result access log
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Reject record, freeze candidate/family and preserve incident evidence
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Reject and invalidate affected scope; suspend project; independent leakage audit; no resume if scope cannot be isolated.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Independent timing audit and incident report
failure_recovery_matrix.protocol_invalidation_rule: YES if exposure scope cannot be isolated

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: NTP/clock telemetry exceeds governed trust state or timestamps conflict
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Fail closed before write
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Fail before write; quarantine interval; restore healthy time quorum; explicit resume under OD-11.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: Existing sealed records remain valid; uncertain interval quarantined
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Clock logs and independent source comparison

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Missed pre-draw heartbeat or expected job absence
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Attempt recovery only before close; otherwise mark missing
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Retry only before T-120 if no valid seal exists; otherwise append missing event and apply OD-05.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: Prior records valid; missed draw has no record
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Job logs, host incident and gap classification

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Semantic unique-key conflict or repeated batch digest
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Reject duplicate idempotently and alert on conflicting payload
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Reject duplicate idempotently; conflicting payload suspends candidate and triggers tamper review.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Uniqueness event and payload comparison

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Normalization or fingerprint field absent
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Reject entire affected batch
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Reject whole batch; no partial eligibility; no post-outcome repair.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Fixed pre-close payload and passing identity audit

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Count differs from expected 3/3/5 or duplicate collapse occurs
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Pause affected candidate and investigate before close
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Suspend candidate; compare actual canonical identities with signed manifest; unexplained drift invalidates affected candidate.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: May remain valid only if actual identities are complete and protocol test handles actual N
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Canonical tickets, reason and independent review

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Manifest digest differs from frozen candidate digest
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Reject capture and disable candidate
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Reject and suspend candidate; restore exact manifest build; exposed interval invalid.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Reproducible build/digest audit

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Endpoint digest mismatch or fixture incompatibility
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Pause capture/evaluation; do not rescore opportunistically
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Pause evaluation/capture as appropriate; restore frozen scorer/evaluator; semantic post-outcome change invalidates protocol.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: Capture may be quarantined; evaluation invalid until frozen scorer restored
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Scorer digest and compatibility fixtures

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Runtime registry identity differs from frozen manifest
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Do not mutate registry; reject and alert
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Reject; do not mutate registry; verify manifest and access logs.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Manifest-to-runtime audit

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Official draw resolver and payload disagree
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Reject batch and pause resolver
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Reject, suspend resolver/project, preserve both source responses; wrong mapping invalidates affected records.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Official schedule snapshot and resolver replay

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Versioned source publishes changed/cancelled draw
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Pause until change is verified before close
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Fail closed until both official sources agree on versioned change before deadline.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: New source digest and owner-approved handling

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Official game rules alter ticket space or endpoint
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Suspend protocol immediately
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Suspend project immediately; owner chooses new future-only protocol or closure; no cross-rule pooling.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: Prior records valid under old rules; future comparability uncertain
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Official rule evidence and statistical impact review
failure_recovery_matrix.protocol_invalidation_rule: YES unless new preregistration governs a separate family

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Schema/version changes without prospective compatibility proof
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Pause writes; preserve pre-migration snapshot
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Pause; reconcile every digest against old read-only store and WORM mirror; rollback on any mismatch.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: Existing records valid only after digest reconciliation
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Migration plan, dual-read comparison and restore proof

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Read error, malformed row or archive checksum failure
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Quarantine store and use only independently sealed copies
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Quarantine store; recover only from verified independent mirror; unrecoverable interval invalid.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: Affected evidence invalid absent verified pre-outcome copy
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Restore and complete digest reconciliation

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Recomputed ticket/batch/export digest differs
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Stop capture and evaluation; preserve both versions
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Stop capture/evaluation; preserve both versions; independent verifier determines isolated scope.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Root-cause report and independent digest match

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Audit log shows unapproved amendment or direct write
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Freeze affected family and revoke access
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Revoke access, suspend affected scope, preserve audit evidence; substantive mutation invalidates.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Access logs and immutable incident report

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Batch/event/ticket counts disagree or transaction failure
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Rollback; retry only before close with same deterministic identity
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Atomic rollback; deterministic retry before T-120 only when no valid seal exists.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: Failed attempt creates no eligible record
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Transaction logs and count reconciliation

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: capture_mode non-live or target already closed
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Reject, alert and preserve rejection event
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Reject and alert; accepted historical row invalidates affected family.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Rejection evidence

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Consecutive or cumulative gaps exceed owner-governed rule
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Remain paused; no historical substitution
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Remain suspended; OD-05 limits decide insufficient-support closure; explicit owner resume only.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: Prior records valid; gaps remain missing
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Outage report and statistical missingness assessment

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: No qualified primary/backup responder for scheduled draw
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Pause before unattended capture if safety cannot be assured
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Backup operator assumes duty if predeclared; otherwise suspend before deadline.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Restored roster and handoff verification

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: No heartbeat or alert telemetry
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Treat system as unsafe and pause
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Treat as unsafe after two missed 60-second heartbeats; suspend and audit recent interval.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Telemetry restoration and gap audit

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Build/runtime cannot generate frozen candidate
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Mark candidate/draw missing; do not replace candidate
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Mark candidate missing, suspend candidate, keep m=3, prohibit substitution.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: Other candidates may remain valid; m stays 3
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Failure logs and owner closure decision

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Runtime/library no longer supported or reproducible
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Freeze upgrades and pause affected component
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Pause affected component; use OD-10 migration or close candidate; no silent upgrade.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Reproducible migration/compatibility evidence

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Unauthorized credential use, code execution or data access
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Revoke access, pause all capture and preserve forensic evidence
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Revoke credentials, suspend project, preserve forensic and archive evidence; resume only if integrity scope proven.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: Affected interval invalid unless integrity/timing independently proven
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Forensic report, key rotation and independent audit

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Result-link timestamp precedes batch seal
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Reject linkage and invalidate affected draw evidence
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Reject link and invalidate affected draw; capture/outcome privilege breach review.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Independent logs and incident report

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Required evidence unavailable after storage event
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Stop analysis and recover from verified archive only
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Stop analysis/capture as needed; restore from verified copy; close if evidence cannot be recovered.
failure_recovery_matrix.record_validity: Lost interval invalid if no verified copy
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Restore proof and digest reconciliation

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Schedule or result authority cannot be verified
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Fail closed; do not use manual source as confirmatory
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Fail closed; no manual confirmatory source; resume after official corroboration returns.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Verified source restoration
failure_recovery_matrix.protocol_invalidation_rule: NO unless unverified data was accepted

failure_recovery_matrix.detection: Capture/export/monitoring credentials unavailable or inconsistent
failure_recovery_matrix.immediate_safe_action: Pause affected operations before close
failure_recovery_matrix.selected_recovery: Use pretested overlapping credentials; on failure suspend affected operation before deadline.
failure_recovery_matrix.required_recovery_evidence: Rotation audit and end-to-end rehearsal

long_horizon_maintenance_plan.horizon: 3605 valid future draw-level observations per candidate; draw count governs
long_horizon_maintenance_plan.ownership: Named primary/backup RACI before G2 with governed handoff and succession.
daily_weekly: Weekly archive reconciliation and alert-quality review
quarterly: sample archive restore and digest verification
dependency vulnerability review without semantic upgrade
long_horizon_maintenance_plan.abandonment_rule: If required primary/backup ownership or minimum resource commitment lapses, suspend; owner chooses restored commitment, HOLD, or scientific closure without efficacy peeking.

missingness_policy.first_miss_action: Immediate candidate-specific suspension; project-wide miss suspends project.
missingness_policy.basis: 0.5% of fixed 3605 horizon, rounded down; selected before activation.
missingness_policy.final_support_rule: Final efficacy requires 3605 valid sealed observations for the candidate. Missing draws are never counted, imputed, replaced, or backfilled. Calendar continuation to obtain fixed valid N is pre-registered, not result-triggered, and ends if the envelope is breached.
missingness_policy.classification_timing: Missing event and infrastructure classification must be timestamped by automated monitoring without outcome inspection; otherwise the gap is unexplained.
missingness_policy.candidate_vs_project: Candidate-only failure suspends/closes that candidate; shared resolver/time/storage/security failure suspends the project. Family m remains 3.
missingness_policy.resume: Explicit incident closure and owner reauthorization before the next eligible draw; no automatic resume.
missingness_policy.closure: Second consecutive miss, nineteenth cumulative explained miss, any unexplained gap, backfill attempt, or outcome-aware classification closes the affected scope as insufficient support or protocol invalidated according to integrity impact.
sensitivity: 1 miss: immediate suspension and incident review

evaluator_design.versions: Evaluator and independent verifier source/container/dependency digests sealed before activation.
inputs: frozen P274A contract and candidate manifest
evaluator_design.checkpoint_50: Integrity/support only; no p-value or success claim.
evaluator_design.checkpoint_300: Integrity plus non-binding conditional-power<10% futility only; no alpha spend or efficacy.
evaluator_design.final: Exactly 3605 valid observations; exact one-sided binomial for constant N or exact Poisson-binomial for varying N; Bonferroni m=3; no optional result-triggered extension.
evaluator_design.duplicate_idempotency: One deterministic candidate/target observation; duplicates rejected; conflicting duplicates invalidate affected scope until resolved.
evaluator_design.independent_verification: Second implementation must reproduce support, q values, critical thresholds, p-values, confidence bounds, and classes.
evaluator_design.migration: Only non-semantic runtime migration with byte-identical fixture outputs; semantic post-outcome change invalidates.

migration_versioning_policy.data_schema: Additive/versioned only; no rewrite of sealed rows.
migration_versioning_policy.evidence_migration: Pause, copy, verify all canonical digests and signatures, retain old store read-only, rollback on mismatch.
migration_versioning_policy.runtime_dependencies: Reproducible locked build; security/platform migration through OD-10 dual verification.
migration_versioning_policy.scorer_evaluator: Semantic version frozen; semantic change requires a new future-only protocol.
migration_versioning_policy.resolver_outcome_sources: Version raw source responses and parsers; dual-run before switching.
migration_versioning_policy.key_rotation: Overlap signatures, retain historical public verification chain.
migration_versioning_policy.authorization: Every migration requires a separately scoped authorization; G1 does not authorize execution.

project_invalidation: accepted resolver/clock uncertainty across all candidates
shared evidence-store tampering without verified mirror
suspension_not_automatic_invalidation: healthy pre-deadline retry after no commit
planned migration completed between capture windows
protocol_invalidation_policy.new_protocol_requirement: Any invalidated family/candidate may only restart under a newly authorized future-only protocol with a new boundary; historical evidence never repairs it.

capture_privileges: read sealed manifest and resolver/time attestation
request signature/write archive through narrow interfaces
evaluator_privileges: read verified exports and links only
security_access_policy.manual_actions: Activation, resume, migration, key rotation, emergency access, and retention change require two authorized approvers and immutable event.
security_access_policy.reviews: Quarterly access review; immediate revocation on role change or incident.

implementation_package_decomposition_for.name: Candidate manifest and reproducible build package
Outcome-blind producer and one-shot capture package
Evidence store, signing, mirror, and export package
forbidden: production DB coupling in recommended profile

implementation_package_decomposition_for.name: Fault, restart, restore, and migration rehearsal package

pre_g2_acceptance_evidence_gates: OD-02: Choose a dedicated evidence DB technology and WORM-capable independent archive
OD-02: Prove atomic append, uniqueness, no overwrite, canonical export, restore, and digest reconciliation on synthetic data
OD-02: Keep the production operational DB outside the evidence trust boundary
OD-03: Document exact official endpoints and manual verification
OD-03: Store raw responses, fetch times, source versions, Asia/Taipei and UTC normalized times
OD-03: Pass cancellation, delay, reschedule, ROC/Gregorian, timezone, and disagreement fixtures
OD-04: At least four configured sources, three healthy, two administrative domains
OD-04: NTS where available; offset <=1 s; spread <=2 s; telemetry age <=60 s
OD-04: Synthetic skew, stale telemetry, source loss, and clock-step tests fail closed
OD-04: Batch sealed by T-120 s
OD-06: Named primary/backup for service, operations, science, security/key custody, outcome linkage, and independent verification
OD-06: Recorded acknowledgment and escalation coverage
OD-06: No incompatible role combination
OD-07: Retention policy: protocol lifetime plus seven years
OD-07: WORM/version-lock proof
OD-07: Quarterly digest sample and annual full restore
OD-07: Documented deletion authority after retention
OD-08: Non-exportable asymmetric key design
OD-08: Capture worker cannot administer or export key
OD-08: Rotation, revocation, compromise, and historical verification fixtures
OD-08: Independent verifier has public material only
OD-10: Reproducible source/dependency manifests
OD-10: Synthetic rehearsal, rollback, dual-read comparison, and all-record digest reconciliation
OD-10: No capture window overlaps migration
OD-10: Old store/runtime retained
OD-12: Signed minimum resource declaration by category and role
OD-12: Annual renewal without efficacy peeking
OD-12: Scientific closure if commitments lapse before G2
AD-01: Manifest includes protocol, candidate, strategy source, dependency/container, scorer digest, expected N, schema, fixtures
AD-01: Three fixture replays byte-identical
AD-01: Capture rejects unknown manifest digest
AD-03: Heartbeat every 60 seconds; critical after two misses
AD-03: Warning at T-10 min, critical if no seal by T-3 min, hard fail at T-2 min
AD-03: Immediate critical on resolver, clock, manifest, digest, storage, access, or capture failure
AD-03: Primary acknowledgment 5 min, backup escalation 10 min; fail closed regardless
AD-04: Role matrix and machine identities per plane
AD-04: No shared write credential; capture write-only, evaluator read-only, linker outcome-append-only
AD-04: Dual approval for activation, resume, migration, key rotation, retention change, emergency access
AD-04: Quarterly access review and immediate revocation
AD-05: Evaluator and verifier built independently with shared frozen fixtures only
AD-05: Exact null, varying-N Poisson-binomial, duplicate/idempotency, missingness, and checkpoint fixtures
AD-05: Evaluator cannot emit efficacy at 50/300
AD-05: Package/container/source digests sealed before activation and reproducible
AD-06: Crash at every write boundary leaves zero or one valid deterministic batch
AD-06: Restart never creates a second valid identity
AD-06: Backups include all required DB/WAL state through supported online backup or quiesced snapshot, never raw-copy ambiguity
AD-06: Quarterly sample restore and annual full isolated restore reconcile all digests
AD-06: Regional/site loss exercise restores read-only evidence before capture resumes
AD-08: Separate official outcome source verification and raw-response preservation
AD-08: Seal barrier proves capture manifest existed before outcome availability
AD-08: Linker append-only and idempotent; cannot update predictions or seals
AD-08: Evaluator accepts only verified linked records

invariants.invariant: no historical row enters prospective evidence

invariants.invariant: correction only through append-only non-substantive annotation; sealed predictions never change
missingness cannot be repaired through backfill

basis: All 14 canonical decisions examined and resolved

G1_exit_evaluation.g2_readiness_recommendation: A separate G2 authorization may be considered only after the recorded pre-G2 acceptance evidence is attached and verified. P274C itself does not authorize G2.
